name: Mondújar and Béznar Reservoir Loop
name_original: Embalse de Béznar — circular desde Béznar — Valle de Lecrín
description: This difficult 16.2-mile mountain biking route circles the Embalse de Béznar, offering stunning reservoir views and orange groves in Lecrín
region_intro: Mountain biking around Lecrín offers diverse landscapes at the foot of the Sierra Nevada mountains. The region features lush, green, orchard-covered terrain, contrasting with snow-capped peaks. Riders can explore extensive pine forests and cultivated lands, including olive, almond, and citrus groves. This varied topography provides a wide network of tracks suitable for off-road cycling. Best mountain bike trails around Lecrín The most popular mountain bike trail is Transnevada Route I: Nigüelas - La Rinconada, a 18.7 miles (30.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 50 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation changes through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Transnevada Route I: Nigüelas - La Rinconada Loop, a difficult 18.4 miles (29.6 km) path. This route winds through the valley's diverse terrain, providing expansive views. Local mountain bikers also love the Mondújar and Béznar Reservoir Loop, a 16.2 miles (26.0 km) trail leading through cultivated lands and offering views of the reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 1 minute. Mountain biking around Lecrín is defined by pine forests, cultivated lands, and Sierra Nevada foothills. The extensive network of tracks offers options for various ability levels, from challenging climbs to more relaxed rides. The routes in Lecrín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Lecrín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of mountain biking terrain can I expect in Lecrín?
A: Lecrín offers a diverse range of terrain for mountain biking, from lush, orchard-covered valleys to extensive pine forests and the foothills of the Sierra Nevada mountains. You'll find a wide network of tracks, including those used by local farmers, providing options for various off-road cycling experiences.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Lecrín?
A: Yes, while Lecrín is known for its challenging routes, there are also trails suitable for beginners. Out of the 84 mountain bike tours available, 4 are classified as easy, offering a gentler introduction to the region's beautiful landscapes.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes in Lecrín?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Lecrín offers numerous difficult routes. A notable option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1185533550/transnevada-route-i-nigueelas-la-rinconada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Transnevada Route I: Nigüelas - La Rinconada&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation changes over its 30.1 km distance. Another demanding choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013440528/mondujar-and-beznar-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondújar and Béznar Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, covering 26 km with nearly 1000 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any loop trails for mountain biking in Lecrín?
A: Yes, Lecrín has several excellent loop trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017928295/transnevada-route-i-nigueelas-la-rinconada-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Transnevada Route I: Nigüelas - La Rinconada Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular difficult option, winding through diverse terrain. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19347725" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Dúrcal – Dúrcal Bridge loop from Béznar&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 38.3 km route.

Q: What can I see along the mountain bike trails in Lecrín?
A: Many trails offer stunning views and pass by interesting natural features. You can encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3823223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de la Luna Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, known for its high walls and large rocks, or the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2777071" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lanjarón River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the bright turquoise Lake Béznar, surrounded by orchards, and the dramatic Sierra Nevada mountains in the background.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Lecrín?
A: Lecrín is a year-round destination for outdoor activities. Its mild climate makes it suitable for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, while summer can be enjoyed by choosing routes that provide shade or are suitable for refreshing stops, such as those near the Moon Ravine in Saleres.

Q: Are there any places to explore or landmarks near the mountain bike routes?
A: Absolutely. Beyond the trails, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4060317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the unique geological formation of the Falla de Nigüelas, a declared Natural Monument. The region's charming villages and cultivated lands, including olive, almond, and citrus groves, also offer scenic points of interest.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Lecrín?
A: The mountain bike trails in Lecrín are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 200 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, from lush orchards to pine forests, and the extensive network of tracks that cater to various skill levels, all set against the stunning backdrop of the Sierra Nevada.

Q: Are there any bike-friendly accommodations or services in the Lecrín Valley?
A: Yes, establishments like the Farmhouse of the Slow in Nigüelas cater specifically to cyclists, providing a strategic base for exploring the valley's routes. Additionally, bike rentals are available in Dúrcal, making it easier to access the trails even if you don't bring your own equipment.

Q: Can I reach mountain bike trailheads in Lecrín using public transport?
A: While the Lecrín Valley has a network of tracks, specific public transport options directly to all trailheads can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for connections to main villages like Nigüelas or Dúrcal, and then plan your route from there, potentially combining with a short ride or taxi to the starting point.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Sierra Nevada peaks?
A: Many routes in Lecrín provide fantastic views of the Sierra Nevada mountains, especially given the valley's position at their foot. On clear days, you can even see all the way to the coast. Trails around Albuñuelas, for instance, offer access to peaks like Herrero (1503m) and Giralda (1430m), which provide rewarding panoramic vistas.
tips: The Béznar Reservoir began its construction in 1977 and will be completed in 1985. It was built in the place called "El Salto del Lobo", a place where the river was encased, narrowing to such an extent that there were rocks between one slope and another that were It said a wolf could jump them, hence its name.
It is a magical place with a beautiful panorama.

faqs.question: How difficult is this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult. While some paths around the Embalse de Béznar can be paved, this specific trail includes segments that are unpaved and more challenging. It requires good fitness, and you might encounter varied terrain including gravel paths, and potentially rocky sections, typical of the broader Sierra Nevada region.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 26 km (16 miles) long and typically takes around 3 hours to complete on a mountain bike. This duration can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You can expect a mix of surfaces, including tarmac and gravel paths. Some sections may be unpaved and more challenging, potentially featuring rocky parts. The diverse terrain adds to the mountain biking experience in the Lecrín Valley.

faqs.question: What notable sights will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy stunning views of the Béznar Reservoir itself. The route also passes by the dramatic Barranco Oscuro, a gorge that adds to the scenic beauty. The surrounding Lecrín Valley is known for its lush landscapes, orange groves, and traditional villages, all set against the backdrop of the Sierra Nevada mountains.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts from Béznar. You should be able to find parking within or near the village of Béznar to begin your ride. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked in the village.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally welcome on trails in the Lecrín Valley, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or near roads. Given the 'difficult' rating and varied terrain, ensure your dog is fit enough for a longer mountain biking excursion.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit is typically during spring (March to May) or autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, and while snow is rare at this elevation, winter can bring colder, wetter conditions. These shoulder seasons offer pleasant weather for mountain biking and vibrant landscapes.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to mountain bike this route around the Embalse de Béznar or within the Lecrín Valley. The area is freely accessible for outdoor activities.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other official trails. You will find sections that overlap with the Andalucía - South Route (Provincia de Granada), the Transandalus, Sendero Forestal - Peñuela, Sendero Pedro Calvo, and Camino Pago de Vadillo.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or pubs near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route starts and ends in Béznar, you'll find amenities in the village. The Lecrín Valley is dotted with traditional villages like Béznar, Melegís, and Restábal, which often have local cafes, restaurants, and pubs where you can refuel before or after your ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain biking trip?
faqs.answer: For this difficult mountain biking route, it's essential to bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, and appropriate safety gear including a helmet. Sun protection (sunscreen, sunglasses) is also recommended, especially during sunnier months. Layered clothing is advisable due to potential temperature changes.
